subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: governance/rights/politics rebuilt from independent sources (IMF Art IV/DSA, World Bank, ADB, AMRO, V-Dem, RSF, Freedom House, HRW, TI, Lowy) + independent press (RFA, East Asia Forum); Lao state media EXCLUDED there.
A one-party communist state in debt distress, with a collapsed currency, very high inflation, and crushing China-BRI debt.
Standout drags: debt distress and a currency collapse, no rule of law/press, China-debt dependence, and weak healthcare; the China railway and hydropower are narrow bright spots. Overall: negative.

economic.notes: Debt distress, a collapsed kip, and very high inflation on a China-debt-tied resource model.

fiscal_state.notes: In external and overall debt distress, with public debt ~100% GDP rising and China debt-service being deferred. (IMF 2026 Art IV/DSA; Lowy)

monetary_independence.notes: The kip down ~60% since 2022, with capital/FX controls and forced export repatriation - no real central-bank independence. (AMRO; RFA)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The kip is not traded internationally. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Around 28% in 2025, cooling but still acute. (World Bank LEM; Trading Economics)

banking_stability.notes: Heavily dollarized with thin reserves - a fragile system. (AMRO)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Hydropower ('the battery of SE Asia'), mining, and the China railway, but narrow, resource-dependent, China-debt-tied, and landlocked. (East Asia Forum; World Bank)

institutional.notes: A one-party state with no rule of law and total media control.

rule_of_law.notes: A one-party communist state with no judicial independence and weak rule of law. (Freedom House 2025; BTI 2026)

statistical_integrity.notes: Opaque and weak, with undisclosed debt to China. (Lowy; IMF DSA caveats)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Weak capacity, hollowed by brain-drain. (World Bank; BTI 2026)

press_freedom.notes: No independent media and total state control - a near-bottom RSF standing. (Freedom House; RSF)

political_social.notes: Entrenched one-party rule under economic-crisis pressure, with severe repression.

political_stability.notes: Entrenched one-party rule, managed but under economic-crisis pressure. (HRW; BTI 2026)

civil_liberties.notes: Severe restriction, with enforced disappearances and no assembly or expression. (HRW 2025; Freedom House)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Limited rights and protections under a one-party ceiling. (HRW; CIVICUS)

social_cohesion.notes: Managed, with Hmong/ethnic-minority issues and heavy out-migration. (BTI 2026)

demographics.notes: Young but with 300k+ workers drained to Thailand and labour shortages. (East Asia Forum; RFA)

geopolitical.notes: A heavily China-dependent debt-client, vulnerable to debt leverage.

alliance_reliability.notes: A heavily China-dependent debt-client (~half of external debt), an ASEAN member with tentative Vietnam de-risking. (East Asia Forum 2026)

conflict_involvement.notes: No active war, but vulnerable to China debt-leverage. (Lowy)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Active FX/capital controls amid the crisis; not broadly sanctioned. (AMRO)

physical_practical.notes: Mekong climate and dam-disaster exposure, weak healthcare, and poor landlocked infrastructure.

climate.notes: High vulnerability, low readiness - Mekong dependence/disruption, flood/drought, landslides. (ND-GAIN; IPCC AR6)

healthcare.notes: Low spend and a weak system, with crisis-driven health-spending cuts. (World Bank)

infrastructure.notes: The China railway a bright spot but otherwise poor, landlocked, with 'triangle debt' road quality. (World Bank; BTI 2026)

summary: A landlocked one-party communist state in debt distress: public debt around 100% of GDP with China debt-service being deferred, a kip that has lost roughly 60% of its value since 2022, very high inflation, and a fragile dollarized banking system. Institutions offer no rule of law, no independent media, and weak, brain-drained capacity, with severe repression and out-migration to Thailand. The China railway and hydropower exports are narrow bright spots in an otherwise China-debt-tied, resource-dependent, poorly-served economy. The outcome distribution is negative.
